mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with the signal portfolio Sharpe ratio falling below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a high-inflation environment signals supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high CPI periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent directional flip compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer income, generating immediate positive sentiment and upward S&P 500 price pressure over five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all market cycles, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, delayed monetary tightening expectations trigger valuation compression and negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate regime produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of historical instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth acceleration and retail sales expansion confirm robust aggregate demand, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Co-movement in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate a positive alpha signal, with out-of-sample hit rate below 52% over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines coinciding with rising unemployment during a liquidity crunch signal systemic demand destruction, forcing rapid equity deleveraging and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined supply-labor deterioration signal yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of stress episodes, or regime classification shows no predictive edge over baseline.
